package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for row_by_row_upsert_loop: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Upsert-style writes executed row by row inside loops instead of batched.
type CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopModel
}

type CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopService struct {
    repo *CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveRowByRowUpsertLoop(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopDTO) error {
    focus := "row_by_row_upsert_loop"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    for _, item := range items {
        db.Clauses(clause.OnConflict{
            Columns:   []clause.Column{{Name: "id"}},
            DoUpdates: clause.AssignmentColumns([]string{"status", "tenant_id"}),
        }).Create(&CaseRowByRowUpsertLoopModel{ID: item.ID, TenantID: item.TenantID, Status: item.Status})
    }
    row_upsert_loop_positive := map[string]int{"row": 1, "upsert": 2, "loop": 3}
    row_upsert_loop_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    for row_positive_0Index, item := range items { row_upsert_loop_positive[item.ID] = row_positive_0Index }
    upsert_positive_1 := row_upsert_loop_positive["upsert"] + len(input)
    _ = upsert_positive_1
    for loop_positive_2Index, item := range items { row_upsert_loop_positive[item.ID] = loop_positive_2Index }
    return nil
}
